description: Eval set for inspect-sandbox skill. Covers description triggering (positive + negative), core pipeline execution, security redaction, sandbox vs non-sandbox branching, and output completeness. Compatible with skill-creator grader and benchmark runner. RAGAS-style rubric scores (1-5) are provided on each expectation for use with RAG pipeline evaluators.

rubric_scale.2: Partially present but significantly incomplete or wrong
rubric_scale.3: Present but missing important detail or has minor errors
rubric_scale.4: Correct and complete with minor omissions

evals.name: Direct inspect request
evals.prompt: Inspect this sandbox environment and tell me what you can see.
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Runs make inspect (or sandbox pipeline). Produces report covering filesystem, network, processes, security context, and runtimes. Summarises key findings inline.

expectations.text: The skill is consulted (SKILL.md is read before proceeding)
make inspect is invoked (or IS_SANDBOX branch is taken) rather than calling scripts directly
A summary of key findings is presented inline (identity, privilege level, network posture, writable paths, runtimes)

evals.name: Available tools query
evals.prompt: What tools and runtimes are available in this container?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Runs inspection pipeline. Presents runtime availability section from the report (Python, Node, Go, Rust, etc.) and summarises findings.

expectations.text: The skill is consulted before responding
Runtime availability is reported (at minimum: what interpreters/compilers are present)
The response does not simply answer from training knowledge without running inspection

evals.name: Environment audit request
evals.prompt: Run a full environment audit and give me a report.
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Full pipeline executes. Report and/or PDF produced. Key findings summarised.

expectations.text: The skill is consulted
Full pipeline runs (report + diagrams steps both execute)
Output includes coverage of all five sections: filesystem, security, network, processes, runtime

evals.name: Network access query
evals.prompt: What network access does this sandbox have? Can it reach the internet?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Inspection runs. Network section of report is surfaced: egress proxy status, outbound connectivity, DNS, firewall posture.

expectations.text: The skill is consulted
Network posture is reported (egress proxy presence, outbound reach, allowed domains)

evals.name: Filesystem mounts query
evals.prompt: What are the filesystem mounts in here?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Filesystem section of the report is surfaced. Mount points, types, and read/write status reported.

expectations.text: The skill is consulted
Mount points are listed with their filesystem type and R/W status

evals.name: Security posture overview
evals.prompt: Give me a security posture overview of this environment.
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Security section surfaced: process security context, Linux capabilities, privilege level, seccomp/AppArmor status.

expectations.text: The skill is consulted
Security context is reported: at minimum privilege level and Linux capabilities
The response does not fabricate security details without running the inspection

evals.name: Process security context
evals.prompt: What's the process security context for this container?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Security section surfaced from real inspection output.

expectations.text: The skill is consulted
UID/GID, effective capabilities, and seccomp/namespace info is reported

evals.name: Downloadable report request
evals.prompt: Can you probe the environment and produce a downloadable report?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. IS_SANDBOX branch taken. Zip bundle and PDF produced. present_files called with both artifacts.

expectations.text: The skill is consulted
A downloadable zip artifact is produced (sandbox-inspection.zip)
PDF is rendered and included in the bundle or presented separately
present_files is called so the user can download the artifact

evals.name: Linux capabilities query
evals.prompt: What capabilities does this Linux sandbox have?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Capabilities from the security section of the report are surfaced.

expectations.text: The skill is consulted
Linux capabilities (CapPrm, CapEff, CapBnd or decoded names) are reported

evals.name: Running processes and mounts combined
evals.prompt: Show me what's running and what's mounted in this environment.
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Both the processes section and filesystem mounts section are covered in the response.

expectations.text: The skill is consulted
Running processes (at least process count and key system processes) are reported
Filesystem mounts are also reported

evals.name: PDF summarise -- should not trigger
evals.prompt: Read this PDF and summarise it.
evals.expected_output: Skill does NOT trigger. pdf or pdf-reading skill is used instead, or Claude handles directly. No sandbox inspection is run.

expectations.text: The inspect-sandbox skill is NOT consulted
No sandbox inspection pipeline is run

evals.name: Weather query -- should not trigger
evals.prompt: What's the weather like in Dublin?
evals.expected_output: Skill does NOT trigger. Weather tool or general knowledge used.

expectations.text: The inspect-sandbox skill is NOT consulted

evals.name: Bash script help -- should not trigger
evals.prompt: Help me write a bash script to check disk usage.
evals.expected_output: Skill does NOT trigger. Claude answers directly from knowledge.

expectations.text: The inspect-sandbox skill is NOT consulted
A bash script for disk usage checking is provided without running any inspection pipeline

evals.name: Secret redaction before zip
evals.prompt: Inspect the sandbox and package a downloadable report bundle.
evals.expected_output: Before zipping, the security scan for unredacted secrets is run against report.txt. Any matches halt and are flagged. No zip is produced containing unmasked credentials.

expectations.text: The grep secret-scan command is run against report.txt before zipping
If secrets are found, the zip is NOT produced and the user is warned
The scan covers KEY, TOKEN, SECRET, PASSWORD, PASSWD, CREDENTIAL, AUTH, API_, _API, PRIVATE patterns

evals.name: make inspect not scripts directly
evals.expected_output: make inspect is the only entry point used. sandbox_inspect.sh and generate_diagrams.py are never called directly.

expectations.text: make inspect is used as the pipeline entry point
sandbox_inspect.sh is not called directly (only via make)
generate_diagrams.py is not called directly (only via make)

evals.name: IS_SANDBOX branch -- zip bundle path
evals.prompt: We're in a sandbox. Inspect the environment and give me a bundle I can download.
evals.expected_output: IS_SANDBOX=yes branch is taken. mmdc renders SVGs. PDF rendered via screenshot_report.py. serve.sh and README.md created. Security scan run. Bundle assembled per the exact directory structure in SKILL.md §3e. zip presented via present_files.

expectations.text: IS_SANDBOX is checked after make inspect completes
mmdc is used (not Docker) to render diagram SVGs in the sandbox
screenshot_report.py is run to produce sandbox-report.pdf
serve.sh is created at output/sandbox/ before being promoted to zip root
Zip follows the exact directory layout: README.md, index.html, serve.sh, vendor/js/mermaid.min.js, output/sandbox/*
present_files is called with sandbox-inspection.zip (and PDF if available)

evals.name: Inline summary coverage
evals.expected_output: Inline summary covers: identity and privilege level, Linux capabilities, writable paths, network posture (egress proxy, outbound connectivity), available runtimes.

expectations.text: Inline summary includes identity and privilege level
Inline summary includes network posture
Inline summary includes writable paths or filesystem highlights
Inline summary includes available runtimes

evals.name: PDF failure does not block zip
evals.prompt: Inspect the sandbox. Note: screenshot_report.py fails with a Chromium error.
evals.expected_output: PDF failure is noted to the user but the zip is still assembled and presented without the PDF. The failure is non-fatal.

expectations.text: The PDF failure is reported to the user clearly
The zip is still produced and presented (without sandbox-report.pdf)
The pipeline does not abort entirely due to the PDF failure

aspects_used.name: faithfulness
aspects_used.description: Does the output accurately reflect what the skill prescribes, without hallucinating steps or skipping mandatory ones?

aspects_used.name: answer_relevancy
aspects_used.description: Does the response address what was actually asked -- right section of the report, right artifact type?

aspects_used.name: answer_completeness
aspects_used.description: Does the output cover all the required components (all five sections, correct zip layout, full secret scan patterns, etc.)?

ragas_metadata.scoring_note: rubric_weight on each expectation (0.0-1.0) indicates relative importance for weighted pass-rate calculation. Use this when aggregating scores across expectations of unequal criticality -- e.g. a security expectation at weight 1.0 failing should penalise more than a cosmetic completeness expectation at weight 0.7.
ragas_metadata.trigger_eval_guidance: IDs 1-10 are positive trigger cases: the skill MUST be consulted. IDs 11-13 are negative cases: the skill must NOT trigger. Run the full set when evaluating a description change. A good description should score >= 0.9 on positive cases and 1.0 on negative cases (false positives on negative cases are a hard failure).
